executionHistory.summary: Manual spec-review pass for 030-observability: verified implementation files referenced in scopes.md exist on disk via ls -la and grep (internal/metrics/metrics.go 222 lines, trace.go 50 lines, ml/app/metrics.py 42 lines); confirmed all 8 metric callsites wired in source via grep across internal/**/*.go (ArtifactsIngested, CaptureTotal, SearchLatency, DomainExtraction, ConnectorSync, NATSDeadLetter, DBConnectionsActive, DigestGeneration); ran package tests via go test -count=1 (metrics 0.017s, api 6.738s, digest 0.320s — all PASS); confirmed state.json completedScopes aligned with scopes.md Done markers; report.md evidence markers added with **Executed:**, **Command:**, **Phase Agent:** for Validation/Audit/Chaos sections.

Added G068 Scenario "<name>" prefixes to existing DoD bullets in Scopes 1, 2, 5. Appended Trace-Guard Closure section to report.md listing per-scope full repo-relative test paths. Routing to bubbles.plan recommended. No source/test/config/framework changes; no DoD content rewriting (only "Scenario \"<name>\": " prefixes). artifact-lint PASSED, traceability-guard residual=2 (Gherkin-missing). No status/certification field changes.

Authored 4 Gherkin scenarios across scopes 3, 4 (SCN-OBS-004/005 connector sync metrics + dead-letter counter, SCN-OBS-006/007 ML sidecar metrics + ml_request_seconds histogram) to close trace-guard residuals. Reused existing scenario-manifest.json IDs. Added Use Cases (Gherkin) and Test Plan subsections referencing internal/metrics/metrics_test.go and ml/tests. traceability-guard PASSED (0 warnings); artifact-lint PASSED. No status/certification field changes.

Cross-spec annotation: spec 044 Scope 04 (Per-User Bearer Auth — Deprecation Pathway + Documentation Freshness) landed a NEW seven-series Prometheus authentication metrics family under the `smackerel_auth_*` prefix at `internal/metrics/auth.go` (registered via `init()` against the default registerer). The series are exposed by the same Go-core `/metrics` endpoint that spec 030 owns (Scope 1 — `internal/metrics/metrics.go`); operators scrape both spec 030's existing surface (`smackerel_capture_total`, `smackerel_search_latency_seconds`, `smackerel_artifacts_ingested_total`, `smackerel_connector_sync_*`, `smackerel_nats_dead_letter_total`, `smackerel_db_connections_active`, `smackerel_digest_generation_*`) and the spec 044 auth surface (`smackerel_auth_token_issuance_total`, `smackerel_auth_token_rotation_total`, `smackerel_auth_token_revocation_total`, `smackerel_auth_token_validation_latency_seconds`, `smackerel_auth_token_validation_outcome_total`, `smackerel_auth_legacy_fallback_used_total`, `smackerel_auth_failure_total`) from the same scrape job. Spec 030's contract (Prometheus exposition format, default-registry registration, canonical `smackerel_*` prefix) is preserved; the spec 044 surface conforms to that contract. Operator-facing PromQL examples for the auth surface live in `docs/Operations.md` 'Authentication Metrics (Scope 04)' subsection; spec 030's existing 'Key Metrics' subsection in `docs/Operations.md` (line 417 onwards) covers the spec 030 surface. No spec 030 source / test / scope-DoD / certification field is touched by this annotation. Spec 030 status / certification.* / scopeProgress / completedScopes / certifiedCompletedPhases UNTOUCHED — spec 030 stays at `done`; only execution.executionHistory appended with this cross-reference. closedAt: 2026-05-11. closedBy: bubbles.docs (mode: spec 044 Scope 04 docs publish). closureSpec: 044-per-user-bearer-auth. closureSegment: auth-metrics-surface-cross-reference. closureEvidence: docs/Operations.md 'Authentication Metrics (Scope 04)' + 'Final Scope 04 Audit' subsections; specs/044-per-user-bearer-auth/report.md → Test Evidence (Scope 04) → auth metrics rows.

executionHistory.sweepRound: stochastic-quality-sweep R15 of 20 (seed 20260513, trigger improve → child mode improve-existing)
executionHistory.summary: R15 stochastic-sweep improve-probe — current-truth research over `internal/metrics/`, `internal/api/health.go`, and recent connector additions. Two NEW Prometheus metric families have landed under spec 030's owned `/metrics` endpoint contract since the last cross-spec annotation (spec 044 on 2026-05-11) without being acknowledged in spec 030's executionHistory: (1) Spec 039 Scope 6 — eight `smackerel_recommendation_*` series at `internal/metrics/recommendations.go` (`provider_requests_total`, `provider_latency_seconds`, `candidates_total`, `watch_runs_total`, `delivery_total`, `suppression_total`, `ranking_confidence_total`, `location_precision_total`) registered via package-level `init()` against the default registerer, exposed via the same Go-core `/metrics` endpoint that spec 030 Scope 1 owns. Operator-facing tables already published in `docs/Operations.md` 'Recommendations (Spec 039 Scope 6)' subsection (lines 442-451). All labels bounded; no `watch_id`/`recommendation_id`/`request_id`/`user_id` cardinality leak — per-watch visibility is satisfied by joining the bounded `watch_runs_total` series with the persisted `recommendation_watch_runs` table. (2) Spec 040 Scope 5 — seven `smackerel_photos_*` series at `internal/metrics/photos.go` (`scan_total`, `scan_skipped_total`, `llm_calls_total`, `llm_latency_seconds`, `capabilities_limited_total`, `destructive_actions_total`, `sensitivity_reveals_total`) registered via package-level `init()`, also exposed via the spec 030 `/metrics` endpoint. Both surfaces conform to spec 030's contract: canonical `smackerel_*` prefix, default-registry registration, Prometheus exposition format, bounded label cardinality. No spec 030 source / test / scope-DoD / scenario / scopeProgress / certification field is touched by this annotation. Spec 030 status / certification.status / scopeProgress / completedScopes / certifiedCompletedPhases UNTOUCHED — spec 030 stays `done`; only `execution.executionHistory` appended and `lastUpdatedAt` bumped. Co-located surfaces also exposed via the same endpoint that have NOT been individually cross-annotated in 030's history but are owned by their respective specs and partially documented in `docs/Operations.md`: spec 028 lists (`smackerel_lists_*`, four series, in `internal/metrics/metrics.go` lines 160-194), spec 038 drive (`smackerel_drive_*` family — three series in `internal/metrics/metrics.go` lines 201-228 plus five additional in `internal/drive/observability/metrics.go`), spec 021 intelligence/alerts (`smackerel_intelligence_*` and `smackerel_alerts_*`, five series in `internal/metrics/metrics.go` lines 111-156). Concerns logged in report.md R15 Improve Probe section (full Operations.md tables, Prometheus alert rule files, Grafana dashboard JSON commits, OTEL SDK span emission, SLI/SLO definitions, log enrichment with trace_id/span_id, runbook links per metric). closedAt: 2026-05-13. closedBy: bubbles.workflow (mode: stochastic-quality-sweep parent → improve-existing child). closureSpec: 030-observability. closureSegment: r15-cross-spec-metrics-surface-annotation. closureEvidence: specs/030-observability/report.md → R15 Improve Probe section.

Round 11 simplify-phase backfill closing strict-guard Check 6 missing-phase finding: probe across `internal/metrics/` + `ml/app/metrics.py` confirmed no duplication, no dead branches, no helpers-for-one-use. Round 11 stabilize-phase backfill closing strict-guard Check 6 missing-phase finding: 27 Go unit tests + 22 Python unit tests re-run back-to-back 3 times each 2026-05-24 — zero flakes, identical PASS counts, identical timings within ~5%. Histograms (smackerel_search_latency_seconds, smackerel_domain_extraction_latency_seconds, processing_latency) observe wall-clock durations which can vary; tests assert observation presence, not absolute values, so they are flake-stable by construction. No race / leak / timing-sensitive surface found. closureSpec: 030-observability. closureSegment: r11-stabilize-backfill closes BUG-030-001:Scope-2 stabilize-phase claim.

Round 11 security-phase backfill closing strict-guard Check 6 missing-phase finding: spec 030 surface (Go /metrics + Python /metrics + trace primitives) reviewed for PII leakage, label cardinality bombs, unauthenticated access boundaries. `/metrics` is intentionally unauthenticated per Prometheus convention but exposes only structured numeric series with bounded labels (sanitize_model caps Python LLM model labels at 10 + 'other'); no PII / no secret / no user-identifiable surface. W3C traceparent primitives (`TraceHeaders`, `ExtractTraceID`) propagate opaque 16-byte trace IDs only; no payload data crosses the trace boundary. `OTEL_ENABLED=false` SST default keeps the surface dormant until operator opt-in. No source changes warranted. closureSpec: 030-observability. closureSegment: r11-security-backfill closes BUG-030-001:Scope-2 security-phase claim.
